violations.text: A sani bucket was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Bucket was removed by staff. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025

Sani bucket was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Inspector removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025
The hand-washing sink in the service area was lacking paper towels. Staff informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (stand mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food debris and grease. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025
Per staff, meatballs and sauce are placed directly into steam table to reheat. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025

meatballs were observed held at 97-101 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff reheated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025

Staff are not completing logs for time as a public health control accurately. Amount prepared, time discarded, amount discarded, and dates were missing on most logs. Inspector discussed proper procedure with staff. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2025

A staff member was observed fluffing their hair at the register area of the service line. Staff continued to touch hair and did not put on a hair net or other effective hair restraint. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Inspector informed PIC. PIC provided hair net. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was 100 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quaternary ammonium sanitizer is 200 ppm. Inspector disposed and refilled bucket to proper concentration, explained to staff to let water run before filling bucket. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -under the pizza conveyor belts -fan of re-heating unit. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

The floors in the walk-in (particularly in the corners) have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024
